color.$description: Primitive color values for the River Romney design system. These are raw values only — no semantic meaning is attached here. Do not reference these tokens directly in UI code; use semantic tokens (color.background.*, color.foreground.*, color.border.*) instead. Primitives exist so semantic tokens have a single authoritative source for each hex value.

green.$description: Single-hue green ramp from near-black to vivid phosphor accent. All dark-theme UI color comes from this family plus two neutrals. Named slots (accent, chip, positive, approve) are used where a value cannot fit the numeric ramp without inversion. Steps 50 and 100 are DE light-mode-only — they do not appear in the base dark theme.

50.$description: Lightest green tint. DE light-mode only — chip/tag fills and callout panels with green emphasis. Not referenced in the base dark theme.

100.$description: Very light green tint. DE light-mode only — success alert/banner backgrounds and subtle success surfaces. Not referenced in the base dark theme.

200.$description: Light sage-white. The brightest text tone in the dark-theme palette — high contrast without the harshness of pure white. Contrast vs green.950: 12.26:1 (AAA). Referenced by: color.foreground.default.

400.$description: Soft desaturated green. Readable and unobtrusive secondary text. Contrast vs green.950: 7.97:1 (AA). Referenced by: color.foreground.alt, color.border.active.

600.$description: Muted sage. The lowest-contrast text color in the system that still meets WCAG AA — used for placeholders, captions, and subtle borders. Contrast vs green.950: 6.32:1 (AA). Referenced by: color.foreground.muted, color.border.hover.

800.$description: Elevated green surface. One step above border-default — used for structural chrome borders, card frames, and prominent dividers. Referenced by: color.border.elevated.

900.$description: Deep forest green. Elevated surface and default border — creates subtle depth above the canvas without breaking the dark aesthetic. Referenced by: color.background.alt, color.background.disabled, color.border.default.

950.$description: Near-black with a warm green undertone. The canvas — every page background and full-bleed section. Warmer than pure black, which reads as sterile. Referenced by: color.background.default, color.foreground.on-action, color.foreground.on-accent.

accent.$description: Terminal phosphor green. The only high-chroma value in the system — vivid and saturated against the muted ramp. High visual weight; always use through semantic tokens. Contrast vs green.950: 11.13:1 (AAA). Referenced by: color.background.action, color.background.accent, color.foreground.action, color.foreground.accent-green, color.foreground.success.

accent-hover.$description: Hover state of the phosphor accent. 16% black mixed into green.accent. Referenced by: color.background.action-hover.

accent-active.$description: Active/pressed state of the phosphor accent. 30% black mixed into green.accent. Referenced by: color.background.action-active.

chip.$description: Dark green chip fill. Named slot — sits between green.900 and green.950 in luminance but cannot be numbered without creating a non-standard step. Used for subtle dark-theme chip/tag fills, callout panel backgrounds, and hairline borders. Referenced by: color.background.accent-green, color.background.success-alt, color.border.muted.

positive.$description: DE light-mode success green. Passes AA on white as text (5.0:1) and supports white text as fill (5.0:1). DE only — not used in the base dark theme. Referenced by: color.background.success, color.foreground.success (DE overrides).

approve.$description: DE Approve outcome green. Teal-shifted green used for Approve badges, income indicators, and boolean type chips in the decision-engine. Distinct from green.positive by its cooler blue-shifted tone. Contrast vs white: 4.55:1 (WCAG AA). DE only — not used in the base dark theme. Referenced by: color.feedback.success (DE override). Renamed from green.teal 2026-04-28.

neutral.$description: Absolute neutrals with no green undertone. Used for edge cases and maximum-contrast needs only. The green palette handles all standard UI color.

white.$description: Pure white. Reserved for maximum-contrast situations such as text overlaid on photography or outside-system contexts. Avoid in standard UI — it reads as outside the palette and breaks tonal cohesion.

black.$description: Pure black. Used as the background.default override for the .art site (photo-optimized, no green undertone needed). Not the default canvas for other properties — that is green.950.

blue.$description: Bright blue ramp. The decision-engine primary action color — a vivid, modern blue chosen over C1 Navy for better visual energy in the enterprise UI.

700.$description: Decision-engine primary action blue. CTA backgrounds, interactive text, accent fills. Contrast vs white: 5.95:1 (WCAG AA).

800.$description: Blue hover state. Applied on interactive elements using blue.700 when hovered.

900.$description: Blue active/pressed state. Applied on interactive elements using blue.700 when actively pressed.

sky.$description: Light blue for dark-theme accent text and icon accents. High contrast on dark canvases. Named slot rather than a numeric step — this sky blue is perceptually lighter than blue.250 and does not belong in the numeric light-mode tint ramp (100→250). Referenced by: color.foreground.accent-blue, color.foreground.info.

100.$description: Very light blue tint. Info alert backgrounds in DE. The lightest entry in the light-mode blue ramp.

150.$description: Slightly more saturated blue tint than blue.100. DE accent.subtle fill — inline highlights, active chip fills, tag backgrounds. Computed from white 86% + blue.700 14%.

200.$description: Medium-light blue tint. DE background.accent-hover — hover fill for accent-tinted interactive surfaces. Computed from white 55% + blue.700 45%.

250.$description: Medium blue for borders on accent-subtle surfaces. DE accent.subtle-border — chip outlines, tag borders, badge strokes. Computed from white 50% + blue.700 50%.

950.$description: Dark blue chip fill. Dark-theme background accent — subtle chip/tag fills, callout panels with blue emphasis. Referenced by: color.background.accent-blue, color.background.info-alt.

red.$description: Red ramp for destructive states and deny outcomes in the decision-engine. Not used in the base dark theme.

500.$description: Destructive action / Deny outcome. Used for delete controls, error states, and Deny rule outcomes in decisioning tables. Contrast vs background.default (#0A0D0A): 4.51:1 (WCAG AA). Numbers swapped 2026-04-28 — was formerly red.600; red.500 is now the lighter of the two action reds. Brightened 2026-05-22 from #D03027 to reach WCAG AA on the dark canvas.

600.$description: Vivid danger/error red. Used for feedback.error and foreground.danger in decision-engine — a deeper, more saturated crimson than red.500. Contrast vs white: 6.01:1 (WCAG AA). Numbers swapped 2026-04-28 — was formerly red.500.

700.$description: Red hover state. Applied on elements using red.500 when hovered.

800.$description: Red active/pressed state. Applied on elements using red.500 when actively pressed.

50.$description: Lightest red — subtle danger surface. Used as background-subtle for danger/error states in light-mode themes. Red.600 text on this background: 4.64:1 (WCAG AA). Not used in the base dark theme.

100.$description: Very light red tint — danger hover surfaces, error row highlights. DE background.danger-subtle. Computed from white 92% + red.600 8% (the deeper crimson). Slightly more saturated than red.50 (#FEF2F2); use for interactive hover signals, red.50 for static alert fills.

purple.$description: Purple ramp for reserved/pending outcome states in the decision-engine. Not used in the base dark theme.

600.$description: Purple accent — reserved for pending/review outcome states in decisioning tables. Contrast vs white: 5.76:1 (WCAG AA).

gray.$description: Cool gray ramp for light-mode surfaces, borders, and text in the decision-engine sub-brand. No green undertone — these are neutral cool grays suited for enterprise data UI on white backgrounds.

50.$description: Lightest surface gray. Used as background.alt in decision-engine — secondary panels, table row backgrounds. Contrast vs gray.900: 12.1:1 (WCAG AAA).

100.$description: Subtle border. Used as border.muted in decision-engine — hairline dividers, subtle separators.

200.$description: Standard border. Used as border.default in decision-engine — input outlines, card edges, table borders.

500.$description: Muted text on white. Used as foreground.muted in decision-engine. Contrast vs white: 5.17:1 (WCAG AA). Previous value #767686 calculated to 4.46:1 — barely fails AA despite the documented 4.57:1 figure. Corrected 2026-04-27.

navy.$description: Secondary text — navy-tinted mid-tone. Used as foreground.secondary in decision-engine. Named slot rather than a numeric step — this navy blue-gray is perceptually darker than gray.700 (#4A4A5A) due to its chromatic shift, making numeric sequencing impossible without inversion. Contrast vs white: 9.39:1 (WCAG AAA).

700.$description: Secondary text on white. Used as foreground.alt in decision-engine. Contrast vs white: 8.9:1 (WCAG AAA).

900.$description: Near-black with a cool cast. Used as foreground.default in decision-engine — primary headings, body copy, labels. Contrast vs white: 16.1:1 (WCAG AAA).

950.$description: Deepest navy. Used as foreground.primary in decision-engine — the darkest text value, for the most prominent labels and headings where maximum contrast is needed. Contrast vs white: 17.39:1 (WCAG AAA).

300.$description: Elevated gray border step for DE light mode. Between border-default (gray.200) and border-hover (blue.700). Used for structural chrome borders — headers, card frames, prominent dividers.

600.$description: Tertiary text on white — sits between foreground.muted (gray.500, 5.17:1) and foreground.alt (gray.700, 8.9:1). Cool blue-gray consistent with the DE palette character. Contrast vs white: ~6.2:1 (WCAG AA). Use for helper/descriptor text that should read as supplemental without the passive 'disabled' feel of muted.

indigo.$description: Indigo ramp for accent-bold fills. Saturated, high-contrast against white. Used for identity surfaces like avatar backgrounds.

700.$description: Saturated indigo for bold accent fills. Contrast vs white: 7.98:1 (AAA). Use via color.background.accent-indigo-bold.

100.$description: Light indigo tint for chip/tag fills. Use via color.background.accent-indigo.

sky.$description: Sky blue ramp for accent-bold fills. Distinct from the blue (navy) ramp — lighter, cooler hue.

700.$description: Saturated sky blue for bold accent fills. Contrast vs white: 5.90:1 (AA). Use via color.background.accent-sky-bold.

100.$description: Light sky tint for chip/tag fills. Use via color.background.accent-sky.

emerald.$description: Emerald green ramp for accent-bold fills. Warmer and more saturated than the base green ramp.

700.$description: Saturated emerald for bold accent fills. Contrast vs white: 5.47:1 (AA). Use via color.background.accent-green-bold.

violet.$description: Violet ramp for base dark theme accent slots. Lighter and cooler than the purple ramp (which serves DE outcome states).

300.$description: Light violet for dark-theme accent text and icon accents. High contrast on dark canvases. Referenced by: color.foreground.accent-violet.

100.$description: Very light violet tint for DE light-mode chip/tag fills and callout panels with violet emphasis. Provisional — value may be refined.

950.$description: Dark violet chip fill. Dark-theme background accent — subtle chip/tag fills, callout panels with violet emphasis. Referenced by: color.background.accent-violet.

amber.$description: Warm amber ramp for base dark theme accent slots. Provides a warm contrast to the cool phosphor green.

300.$description: Warm amber for dark-theme accent text and icon accents. Very high contrast on dark canvases. Referenced by: color.foreground.accent-amber.

500.$description: Medium amber. Warning fill in light-mode (DE). Dark text required: #0A0D0A on this = 6.8:1 AA.

600.$description: Saturated amber for bold accent fills — avatar backgrounds, identity badges. Contrast vs white: 5.02:1 (AA). Use via color.background.accent-amber-bold.

700.$description: Dark amber. Warning text in light-mode (DE). On white: 7.1:1 AA+.

100.$description: Very light amber tint. Warning alert backgrounds in DE.

950.$description: Dark amber chip fill. Dark-theme background accent — subtle chip/tag fills, callout panels with warm amber emphasis. Referenced by: color.background.accent-amber, color.background.warning-alt.

50.$description: Lightest amber tint. DE light-mode chip/tag fills and callout panels with warm emphasis. Lighter and less saturated than amber.100.
